ogTitle: Wedge Island vs Wedgecarrup: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Wedge Island and Wedgecarrup.
heading: Wedge Island vs Wedgecarrup — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Wedge Island, Western Australia and Wedgecarrup, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Wedgecarrup has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Wedge Island (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Wedge Island or Wedgecarrup safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Wedge Island has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Wedgecarrup scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Wedge Island scores 39/100 for bushfire risk. Wedgecarrup scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Wedge Island or Wedgecarrup?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Wedgecarrup has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.55% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
